title: Shrinkflation Is Stealing $2,900 From Your Groceries
hook: Your grocery price went up — but your grocery bag got lighter. Shrinkflation quietly drains $2,900 from the average household every year.
angle: Shrinkflation combines two hidden forces — smaller packages and higher prices — into a 49% effective price hike since 2020. Most consumers only notice one or the other, never both together, making the true cost completely invisible.
audience: Anyone who buys groceries and feels like their money goes less far without understanding why
key_numbers: Grocery prices rose 26% in the same period
$2,900 extra per year for the average household
